CREATE TABLE employees (id INT, name VARCHAR(100), department VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE sales (id INT, employee_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2));
SELECT row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY amount DESC) AS row_num, e.name, s.amount FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id;
SELECT row_number() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS row_num FROM employees;
SELECT row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY id) AS row_num FROM employees;

SELECT e.name, (SELECT sum(amount) FROM sales WHERE employee_id = e.id) as sales_total, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY (SELECT sum(amount) FROM sales WHERE employee_id = e.id) DESC) as rank FROM employees e;
SELECT row_number() OVER () AS row_num, s.amount FROM sales s ORDER BY s.amount ASC;

SELECT e.name, COALESCE(s.total_sales, 0) AS total_sales, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY total_sales DESC) AS rank FROM employees e LEFT JOIN (SELECT employee_id, SUM(amount) AS total_sales FROM sales GROUP BY employee_id) s ON e.id = s.employee_id;
